text.title: Principle Audit Procedure
text.summary: PASS/WARN/FAIL audit protocol for the six PMBOK 7 principles, weighting procedure, and agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Force the audit to identify at least one WARN or FAIL; an all-PASS audit is a signal of bias, not health.
rule.rationale: LLMs and humans both mark every principle PASS to be agreeable; a mandatory non-PASS prevents the audit from becoming a rubber stamp.

rule.statement: Separate the audit step from the decision step; do not let the same agent or person both review and recommend in one turn.
rule.rationale: Combined review-and-recommend produces confirmation bias: the reviewer unconsciously justifies their pre-formed recommendation through the audit.

list.item: PASS: principle is fully honored with evidence; no remediation needed
WARN: principle is partially honored; a specific gap exists with a documented mitigation plan
FAIL: principle is not honored; no mitigation plan; decision should be reconsidered

example.code: Audit this <plan|decision|ADR> against the PMBOK 7 six core principles. For each principle output: PASS / WARN / FAIL + one-sentence evidence. End with the single highest-risk principle violation and one concrete mitigation. Force at least one WARN or FAIL; if all pass, explain why with specific evidence.

section.title: Weighting Procedure
section.p: Weight principles 1-5 per project type and document in constitution.md. Weighting is not permanent — re-weight at major project milestones.

list.item: Regulated medical product: Embed Quality 5, Lead Accountably 5, Holistic View 4
Internal hackathon: Focus on Value 5, Build Empowered Teams 4, others 2-3
Sustainability-mandated public project: Integrate Sustainability 5, Lead Accountably 4

rule.statement: Name one accountable human per principle; principles without owners drift regardless of weighting.
rule.rationale: A highly weighted principle without an owner is still nobody's job; accountability converts weight into action.

section.title: Gate Calibration
section.p: Running the audit on every issue creates noise and reduces compliance. Gate principle audits to decisions that cross significance thresholds:

list.item: Scope changes exceeding X% of baseline (set per project in constitution.md)
Architecture decisions affecting more than one team

antipattern.description: Agent synthesizes the audit and the recommendation in the same prompt turn.
antipattern.reason: Produces confirmation bias; the reviewer unconsciously justifies their pre-formed recommendation through audit language.

antipattern.description: Agent equates "Embed Quality" exclusively with adding tests.
antipattern.reason: Process quality (review rigor, observability, runbooks) is equally within scope; test-only framing misses systemic quality gaps.

antipattern.description: Agent skips "Integrate Sustainability" because it seems irrelevant to tech work.
antipattern.reason: Cloud carbon, data retention periods, and accessibility all count as sustainability dimensions in PMBOK 7; skipping produces an incomplete audit.

text.reference: Emits a six-row markdown audit table for a decision
